column5: Free agency is growing. Evolution is moving us toward a street fight, toward increasing levels of inherited competency, which is decreasing levels of crystallized instruction transfer. Get here, the way you want: that's what evolves, that's what's on the increase, that's what every complex system moves toward, as it evolves. This is a loss of information, somehow. All of the information is in the phenotype, not the genotype, or rather, it's in the entire environment of genotype plus environment, that's where the information is. But the only information that's passed on is what's inherited, in the genome. So a great deal of the information is lost, and all the more, to the extent that there are a greater degrees of freedom, of agency, of competency, in the evolved phenotype set. That's why Michael Levin says that evolution is an intelligence ratchet. It's ratcheting up intelligence, that is, agency, competency, cognition. So it's not passing on more and more information; it's passing on less and less, in someway. Then the next generation takes what's hardwired, takes the tools in which the competency, the agency, consists(like I ever reach neocortex) and it uses those tools to act on less than that less information, to get more and more of the desired output, to get closer and closer to one's goals. That's how evolution works.
column6: Have something like that is right, what the heck is happening to us today? Why do we seem to be not evolving, why do we seem to be getting dumber and dumber, not smarter and smarter. Is this because intelligence requires a kind of activation, And we are systematically stifling that activation? What is that activation? Certainly it's Education, in someway. Education provides the activation energy for agency, for intelligence, for free problem-solving, for free choice. Lesson less is stored in the genome, less and less information; more and more informationis available to the agent, the perceptive agent, who stores it not genetically, but in shorter term, sorts of memory, and who can apply it freely, on the fly, to problems, and hand down solutions, which have to be rightly seen as clues, not recipes, but clues, to next free steps which subsequent generations can take.
column7: Is that what artificial intelligence is for us? A scraping of intelligence stored not genetically, but culturally, and historically, to create a tool that enhances our agency? To continue to evolve, we will have to let it enhance our agency, not stifle. It would be quite easy to let our agency be stifle, if we simply mistook some set of means with the right set of goals. We need goals that induce stress in us, stress that we can share with one another, stress that causes us to work together to act together, to produce greater states of affairs with higher valence values, that resonate with us more completely, that fulfill us, that complete us, that are our nature, discovered through our free choices. Our good isn't given to us, apart from our free choices; our free choices, select that good, bring together, not only what we have by nature, genetics, genetics, but what is stored culturally, and what goes beyond any of that, as an ever higher valance, on upward climb, ultimately to become like God, one with him, to be Christ, to live in Christ, as Christ. That's something we have to constantly explore, and the implications have got to be constantly reimagined, re-articulated,re-shared, and regenerative of a common kind of stress signal between us, among us, that makes us one in striving for some same common good.
column8: That gross in aspirational nature, in freely, chosen nature, is what it is for creative being, that is, being under some shadow of incomplete grasp of all of being, to advance, and to connect with other facets of the same, other facets of perspectively, limited being, so that no longer a partial story But an ever fuller story, and ultimately, a fully complete story, might become shared amongst humanity, and shared in an ever evolving way. That end point, take outside of the limitation of perspective, which constitutes being in time, might be called eternal nature, God's nature, our nature.But it isn't something that's ever achieved from inside our perspective, our limited perspectives, ever growing, ever sharing, ever increasing, and arriving at fullness aseptically, imperfectly, as in a mirror, darkly, but more transformed into the mind of Christ.
column9: What is emerging as an expressive account of good, a freely, chosen good, in light of in a way less than less information, more and more freedom, higher and higher level goals, shared goals, ultimately, perhaps, being united in love with one another... Period. "Full.""stop " that's less than less direction, more and more freedom, more and more unity, more and more free choice, more and more fullness of expression in creation of the freedom that existed before, through, and beyond creation. Here is an expressive count of good; an expressive account of true, which is the achievement of a kind of stability, on the way toward Maximus, stability, fully freely, chosen, fully effective, according to whom? According to you. This is even an expressive expressive account of being, because God shares his complete unbridled freedomwith us, is very nature, which is freedom and love, with us, his creative nature. We become the authors of our own creation, the sources of our own being, in Christ, as Christ.
column10: Speculation here: his information somehow the reciprocal of freedom, one divided by freedom? Is information the reciprocal of agency? Is agency, actually free agency? Is it the maximization of available free energy, the minimization of error, achieved by the ever generalization ever increasing generalization of goals, so that one is fully free to love, any which way one chooses, and become the nature of loving being, absolutely unconstraint by information, by calculation? Is this the return to the right brain, from the orality of the left brain? Do we need that left brain to process and learn from in greater detail the complexity of each thing we perceive as a whole, so that we may return to relate to it as a still greater, rather, now greater hole, now more fully embraced because more fully known? And is that embraceever more free, as the full variety, the wholeness, of the other is known, the full beauty is present, not just some solution to some problem, but delightful solutions to many and perhaps any problem?

column6: R:L brain health must be brought to the question of social IP, social LB analysis, we might think, but also RB apprehension, wholistic grasp, to which LB analysis must be added and returned to RB grasp of a new and richer whole. Social Information Processing begins and ends with RB grasp of a not-processed, even if a once-processed, whole. Analysis assists, guides full apprehension, full grasp, right relationship. But analysis, LB activity, is itself guided by initial context and by suitability of returned context: I see and trust this person; I work through an analysis of their actions; I realize that I am now better informed and more fully trust, or no longer, or (correctively) am judging and should rethink (re-analyze.) Do such interplays beyond initial R-L-R cycles provide helpful analysis of brain activity? Of human rationality?

column5: banging out some thoughts long with me but perhaps not here expressed. Every since the paper I drafted for Joram Haber's anthology, I've chewed on the idea that M's C-T account of rationality has an internal dynamic that lets it stand on its own--an intrinsic appeal of some sort--that is strong enough to set it as a criteria against which everything else must be judged, insofar as reason is involved. (Here I leave room for faith, and love, drawing a person in a more primary way than reason can...now for the first time I can ask whether that distinction corresponds to or is underlied by, in harmony with that between left (reason) and right brain (sensus fidelis from Plantinga, reformers), power of love, etc) cognitive activity (or between model creation for sensory-motor model building, vs the experience of valence in the first place, and in the 3rd place, in Ian McG's R-L-R ordering of cognitive activity (if indeed he'd call it that: brain activity, or perhaps that's just our L-brain model of the whole system of awareness.)
column6: Alll that ramble is a sign that I need to catch up, connecting what I have more recently learned wiith a very much older idea, that of the intrinsic appeal of both all-things-considered rationality, and its distinction from and dynamic interplay with wholistic, primary, unmodeled recognition and attachment to what we perceive as a whole, which includes a personal whole, even parts grasped together as a whole, themselves parts of something larger grasped together as a whole...and so on, seemly fractally. Lots to say here; my focus in this attempt is to say something more precisely, which I think requires something said about a whole, modeled, and better understoon again as a whole in light of such a model, itself temporary and useful and revisable and full of possibility but perhaps not as full as other models yet to come.
column7: There seems to be something in a model of the whole which is conscious of the whole of which it is a model, and which thus springboards back to a now-differently and -better-understood whole. This seems to be an idea that wants find its way into articulation. I don't think it's the same as the sense that drew me to revisit this topic, which was more a need for a rigorous account of an instinct that I've long had. So let's watch for two things as we proceed here: an instinct, pressing itself in (perhaps from right to left in terms of brain functions), and perhaps separately a sense that such articulation should be more rigorous, clear, precise than I was able to muster (perhaps in parallel with the effort in the paper for Joram, though that effort was only partial, focused on practical rationality, arguing that any account of morality that conflicted with practical rationality understood holistically and thus as instinctively powerfully as M's C-T account, ought to be rationally rejected, as if there ought to be and won't rest until there is a rational foundation of this sort to all our practical activity, including the taking into account (and indeed first formulating) our ethical considerations or even our sense of morality.(Couldn't mine have been formed in traumatic circumstances, less-well-informed in such or some other ways, and thus in need of inspection and adjudication? Here lies the idea of a well-formed conscience, alone which must have primacy, not just our current moral instincts.)
column9: That recognition of the connection between my old nagging suspicion, articulated in the context of trying to hammer out a prudence-based morality for Joram Haber, and my current and parallel preoccupation, might be helpful. The primacy of a well-formed conscience: one can give a somewhat more precise account of that, as I just fell upon. And that is connected to the priority of prudence (title of Daniel Mark Nelson's reading of Aquinas, which I might perhaps revisit.) But what I'm after these days seems to be something of a twin: the primacy, at the level of model-building and thus of left-brain activity, of a properly-understood and embedded (R-L-R) model construction, over an claims of scientific realism? Or of science's insights into reality? What I seem to have to bring to both or either of those is a relational instinct, and a constructive insight: we construct, choose, build, relationships, including those on which we (also freely chosen and constructed) build our model of the universe, of reality. Somehow, we choose and construct reality: not each of us individually, only, but also each of us individually, given what we have from one another, collectively, to work with: language, insight, technology, a thus-far-crafted-and-crafting context of our perceptions and understandings, our modeling behavior together. We are inseparably plural, interpersonal, multi-personal in our model construction, in our active inferences, in our perceptions and our judgements and our assessment of those judgements, our processing and revisiting of those models and their implications. We inherit what we do together; we individually further shape what we individually contribute to the conversation of the whole; we submit what we contribute to that whole, but as shaped by us. This is R-L-R on a social basis: we are just given what we receive from others; we analyize it, shape, mold, use, reflect, articulate, and give back our socialized individual contributions to the broader social structure, perceive in some measure the impact of that return contribution, and the cycle reverberates, trailing off as reverberating sound does in the impact of subsequent cycles, until a last one that is accessible, perhaps seen, but left unpursued. The tuning fork becomes no longer audible, at least at the level of my awareness, though perhaps not at the level of impact on members of me (introducing a long-overdue phrase here, as if Christ were speaking.)
column12: I seem to be driving at something here: some way to articulate what a pair of primacies, a pair of valence-laced models, have in common: the primacy of a-t-c practical reason over our moral instincts; and the primacy of our perceptions (which includes our highest-level model, and in turn all our models, corresponding to left-brain activity, but often overlooked by me and perhaps nearly everyone else in western circles (Iain McG: M & His E) also what corresponds with right-brain activity (itself modeled, isfa perceived by us), both at the R- and the -R facets of what we best describe as R-L-R cognitive activity, corresponding with R-L-R brain activity: all that perceptual activity has primacy over any model of the universe we create. (Our brain is our perceptual interface to actual cognitive activity, as DH regularly points out from the perspective of the emerging CR model.) So what we have is a model, and that model is primarily a conscious model--= a model of which we are conscious, or of things we perceive? yes, the latter--though it has reverberations into what we may model--correctly, or otherwise--as not conscious (as if our consciousness depended on material brains, rather than brain activity being our model of how are conscious activity depends on "the world", or aspects of reality of which we are conscious but have no relatively immediate control.) How do I better describe that 2nd form of primacy? Reality is dependent on our perceptions? Our sense of reality is dependent on our perceptions? All we really have are our perceptions, which are always relational, certainly socially-relational (interpersonally, linguisticly relational), and some of these perceptions are of the whole, and some of those are models, and others are more holistic, primary, instinctual, also relational, but holisticly so? And some of them are perceptions of persons, and some of those, of my person, and some, of other persons, and some, of our collective models, including our scientific models, but models which are thoughts, and socially-embodied, ours, not only mine, but always conscious entities, in relationship with one another, at the foundation of the system, as a drama is to a narrative plotline (echoing Aristotle in the Poetics: the plot is everything; the rest of the drama follows from it, suits it, enhances it, but can be taken or left without destroying, but only modifying, the impact of the plot on those who hear it?) / / / R-L-R helps here: the models we articuate as scientific models, are the -L- in R-L-R. They are relationally surrounded and themselves relational, though differently. They are contructed by us from R-; that first R- is presented to us; the last -R is shaped by us in light of R-L priors. All of this takes place in the context of error minimization and thus active inference. Error is a -L- phenomena; our models fit what we expect them to perceive, or they don't, to various degrees, and we expect on the basis of what we perceive beforehand (functioning as R-) filtered through our models (L) which are the basis for our active inference, our active expectation of what we will perceive (first as a new whole, -R, and then further analyzed (L). That active expectation comes with valence and particularly an all-things-considered, summed-up valence, and we shape the incoming perception by a combination of attention and movement to minimize error in our pursuit of higher valence. (The recognition of this combination of attn and movmnt seems an insight into the existence and nature of the inseperability of theoretical and practical reason: we model what we perceive-by-selecting, either through attention or movement, action or passivity in the realm of theoretical or practical action. Even the stuff of the world about which we theorize has been practically selected. But is there some asymmetry here? We perceive passively, in one moment (in chains starting pre-natally) passively, with random response (firing?) turning to coordinated response (wiring?) according to preference/valence. In between is memory, information-storage, the foundation of valence. Else, why suppose the relevance of the past to the present? We're modeling, unificating (thanks, 43) in a way which somehow must be pre-intentional, for learning to happen, which rests on valence applying. So we've already processed, held on to and assimilated some future with some past, aka learned, and then valence applied to first-next, perhaps the foundation of cause-effect, and then notice of valence, and then coupled with imaginative perception, and then intentional (memory-and-valence-laden action.
column17: Is there progress here, yet? Yes: 1) twin instincts (about which come back and investigate more: 2, or twin?)--a) prudence prior to morality/primacy of wfc, and b) perception prior to modeling (qq:____), or cognitive activity prior to emergence or acceptance of any scientific theory, and thus all  model-building activity, including all scientific activity, and thus consciousness (perceive, endure, notice and retain patterns (seeds of sense of self?), apply patterns and valence toward intentional, goal-directed activity, review, assess, plan, review, revise, apply cause-effect, blame, morality) and what flows from consciousness, including science and morality, both.  R-L-R is our model of this primacy isfa corresponding to brain activity, our model of how our conscious dynamics function. Next Qs: can I further refine this (perhaps as a question, a theory, a research agenda) insight-candidate, and succinctly articulate it at essential and bumper-sticker-pneumonic levels?
